name: IFRS S1
title: General Requirements for Disclosure of Sustainability-related Financial Information

sections.title: Governance
sections.description: Requires disclosure of the governance processes, controls, and procedures used to monitor, manage, and oversee sustainability-related risks and opportunities. Covers board and management oversight responsibilities and how sustainability considerations are embedded in organizational governance.
requirements: Entities must disclose the governance body or individual responsible for oversight of sustainability-related risks and opportunities, including their position within the organizational structure.
The processes and frequency by which the governance body is informed about sustainability-related risks and opportunities must be described, including reporting lines and escalation procedures.
Controls and procedures in place to monitor sustainability risks and opportunities must be disclosed, including how they are integrated into existing governance frameworks.
Delegation of sustainability-related responsibilities to management-level positions must be described, including specific roles, authority levels, and accountability mechanisms.
How the governance body considers sustainability-related risks and opportunities when setting entity strategy, making major transactions, and overseeing risk management must be disclosed.

sections.title: Strategy
sections.description: Requires disclosure of the sustainability-related risks and opportunities that could reasonably be expected to affect the entity's prospects, including their impact on the business model, value chain, strategy, financial position, and financial performance. Includes resilience assessment requirements.
requirements: Entities must disclose sustainability-related risks and opportunities that could reasonably be expected to affect their cash flows, access to finance, or cost of capital over the short, medium, and long term.

The current and anticipated effects of sustainability-related risks and opportunities on the entity's business model and value chain must be described, including where in the value chain risks and opportunities are concentrated.
How sustainability-related risks and opportunities have affected the entity's strategy and decision-making must be disclosed, including any transition plans adopted.
The current and anticipated effects of sustainability risks and opportunities on financial position, financial performance, and cash flows must be quantified where practicable.
Entities must assess the resilience of their strategy and business model to significant sustainability-related uncertainties, using scenario analysis where appropriate.
Trade-offs between sustainability-related risks and opportunities that the entity considered in developing its strategy must be disclosed.

sections.title: Risk Management
sections.description: Requires disclosure of the processes used to identify, assess, prioritize, and monitor sustainability-related risks and opportunities, and how these processes are integrated into the entity's overall risk management framework.
requirements: Entities must disclose the processes used to identify sustainability-related risks and opportunities, including the scope of risks considered and data sources used.
The processes used to assess the likelihood and magnitude of sustainability-related risks must be described, including prioritization methodology and materiality thresholds.
How sustainability-related risks are monitored on an ongoing basis must be disclosed, including key indicators, review frequency, and responsibility assignment.
The extent to which sustainability-related risk management processes are integrated into the entity's overall risk management framework must be described.
Changes to sustainability risk management processes compared to prior reporting periods must be disclosed, including the rationale for any modifications.

sections.title: Metrics & Targets
sections.description: Requires disclosure of quantitative and qualitative metrics used to measure, monitor, and manage sustainability-related risks and opportunities. Includes industry-specific metrics derived from SASB Standards and disclosure of targets set by the entity, including progress against those targets.
requirements: Entities must disclose metrics used to measure and monitor sustainability-related risks and opportunities, including how those metrics are defined, calculated, and validated.
Industry-specific metrics must be disclosed in accordance with applicable SASB Standards, reflecting sector-relevant sustainability performance indicators.
Targets set by the entity for sustainability-related risks and opportunities must be disclosed, including the timeframe, base period, interim milestones, and scope of each target.
Progress against each disclosed target must be reported, including quantitative performance data and explanations for any significant deviations from planned trajectories.
Where applicable, baseline data and the methodology used to determine baselines must be disclosed, enabling users to assess trends and performance over time.
Metrics must be reported for the current period and at least one comparative prior period, with restatements disclosed when measurement methodology changes.
